steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which quotation shows Winnemucca directly addressing the reader to explain a custom. Both elements are required: a direct address to the reader and an explanation of a cultural practice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"During the time my grandfather was away in California, where he [stayed] till after the Mexican war, there was a girl-baby born in our family."': No direct reader address and no explanation of a custom — just a family anecdote. ✗
  • Choice B — '"Now, my dear reader, there is no word so endearing as the word father, and that is why [my people] call all good people father or mother."': Directly addresses the reader ('my dear reader') and explains why Paiutes call good people 'father' or 'mother' — a cultural custom. ✓
  • Choice C — '"But how can I describe the scene that followed? Some of you, dear readers, can imagine."': Addresses readers but explains nothing about a custom. ✗
  • Choice D — '"We would all go in company to see if the flowers we were named for were yet in bloom, for almost all the girls are named for flowers."': Mentions a custom (flower naming) but contains no direct address to the reader. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
